w13.title: Electrical kitchen appliances & conservation of energy (1)
w13.topic: Energy use of kettle, toaster and microwave oven; conservation of energy in kitchen appliances.
w13.lo1: Explain the Law of Conservation of Energy in kitchen appliances.
w13.lo2: Use the formulae P = V × I, E = P × t, and P = V × I × cos φ.
w13.lo3: Calculate energy consumption and cost for kettles, toasters and microwaves.
w13.lo4: Identify the power factor of resistive vs. non-resistive loads.

theory.title: Theory — Conservation of Energy & Electricity
theory.intro: The Law of Conservation of Energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed from one form to another.
theory.acIntro: In AC circuits voltage and current may not be in phase, therefore:
theory.acNote: The power factor (cos φ) indicates efficiency: cos φ = 1 → purely resistive (kettle, toaster); cos φ < 1 → losses exist (motors, electronics).

kettle.name: 1 — Kettle
kettle.howBody: A kettle converts electrical energy into heat using the Joule effect. Current flows through a resistor, the resistance produces heat, and water reaches 100 °C and boils.
kettle.phys1: Water has high specific heat — needs more energy
kettle.calcBody: Power = 2 kW. Time = 5 min. Using E = P × t: E = 2 × (5/60) ≈ 0.166 kWh. At €0.30/kWh that is ≈ €0.05.

toaster.name: 2 — Toaster
toaster.howBody: A toaster produces heat using the Joule effect. Heat transfers to the bread by conduction, convection and radiation.
toaster.calcBody: Power = 1 kW. Time = 10 min. Using E = P × t: E = 1 × (10/60) ≈ 0.166 kWh. At €0.30/kWh that is ≈ €0.05.

micro.name: 3 — Microwave Oven
micro.howBody: A microwave oven uses electromagnetic waves. The magnetron generates waves that make water molecules vibrate; friction between molecules produces heat. This is called dielectric heating.
micro.phys4: No metal objects may be placed inside — metal reflects microwaves
micro.calcBody: Power = 1.2 kW. Time = 10 min. Using E = P × t: E = 1.2 × (10/60) ≈ 0.20 kWh. At €0.30/kWh that is ≈ €0.06.

quiz.title: Quiz — multiple choice (15 questions)
quiz.intro: Choose the correct answer for each question. Q1–5: kettle. Q6–10: toaster. Q11–15: microwave.
